A **Search Page with Highlighting** is a very common **Frontend System Design / React interview** question.

The goal is:

✅ Search through a list of items\
✅ Highlight matching text\
✅ Debounce user input\
✅ Support keyboard navigation\
✅ Handle large datasets efficiently

***

# Architecture

```text
Search Input
      ↓
Debounce
      ↓
Filter Results
      ↓
Highlight Matches
      ↓
Virtualized List (optional)
```

***

# Example UI

```text
Search: rea

Results:

✓ React JS
✓ React Native
✓ Learning React
```

Highlighted output:

```text
[Rea]ct JS
[Rea]ct Native
Learning [Rea]ct
```

***

# Data

```ts
const data = [
  "React JS",
  "React Native",
  "Angular",
  "Vue",
  "Learning React",
  "JavaScript",
];
```

***

# Highlight Utility

This is the most important interview piece.

```tsx
function highlightText(
  text: string,
  query: string
) {
  if (!query) return text;

  const regex = new RegExp(
    `(${query})`,
    "gi"
  );

  return text
    .split(regex)
    .map((part, index) =>
      part.toLowerCase() ===
      query.toLowerCase() ? (
        <mark key={index}>
          {part}
        </mark>
      ) : (
        part
      )
    );
}
```

***

# Search Component

## SearchPage.tsx

```tsx
import React, {
  useMemo,
  useState,
} from "react";

const items = [
  "React JS",
  "React Native",
  "Angular",
  "Vue",
  "JavaScript",
  "Learning React",
];

function highlightText(
  text: string,
  query: string
) {
  if (!query) {
    return text;
  }

  const regex = new RegExp(
    `(${query})`,
    "gi"
  );

  return text
    .split(regex)
    .map((part, index) =>
      part.toLowerCase() ===
      query.toLowerCase() ? (
        <mark key={index}>
          {part}
        </mark>
      ) : (
        part
      )
    );
}

export default function SearchPage() {
  const [searchTerm, setSearchTerm] =
    useState("");

  const filteredResults =
    useMemo(() => {
      return items.filter((item) =>
        item
          .toLowerCase()
          .includes(
            searchTerm.toLowerCase()
          )
      );
    }, [searchTerm]);

  return (
    <div className="search-page">
      <input
        type="text"
        placeholder="Search..."
        value={searchTerm}
        onChange={(e) =>
          setSearchTerm(
            e.target.value
          )
        }
      />

      <ul>
        {filteredResults.map(
          (item) => (
            <li key={item}>
              {highlightText(
                item,
                searchTerm
              )}
            </li>
          )
        )}
      </ul>
    </div>
  );
}
```

***

# CSS

```css
.search-page {
  width: 500px;
  margin: 40px auto;
}

input {
  width: 100%;
  padding: 12px;
  margin-bottom: 20px;
}

mark {
  background: yellow;
  color: black;
  font-weight: bold;
}
```

***

# Add Debouncing

Avoid filtering on every key stroke.

## useDebounce Hook

```tsx
import {
  useEffect,
  useState,
} from "react";

export function useDebounce(
  value: string,
  delay = 300
) {
  const [debouncedValue,
         setDebouncedValue] =
    useState(value);

  useEffect(() => {
    const timer = setTimeout(() => {
      setDebouncedValue(value);
    }, delay);

    return () => clearTimeout(timer);
  }, [value, delay]);

  return debouncedValue;
}
```

Usage:

```tsx
const debouncedSearch =
  useDebounce(searchTerm, 300);
```

***

# Search API + Highlighting

Real-world scenario:

```text
Frontend
    ↓
Search API
    ↓
Elastic Search
    ↓
Search Results
    ↓
Highlight Matches
```

Example response:

```json
[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "title": "React JS Tutorial"
  },
  {
    "id": 2,
    "title": "Learning React"
  }
]
```

Frontend applies highlighting.

***

# Advanced Highlighting

Highlight multiple occurrences.

```tsx
function highlight(
  text: string,
  query: string
) {
  if (!query) return text;

  const regex = new RegExp(
    query,
    "gi"
  );

  const matches = [];
  let lastIndex = 0;

  text.replace(
    regex,
    (match, offset) => {
      matches.push(
        text.slice(
          lastIndex,
          offset
        )
      );

      matches.push(
        <mark key={offset}>
          {match}
        </mark>
      );

      lastIndex =
        offset + match.length;
    }
  );

  matches.push(
    text.slice(lastIndex)
  );

  return matches;
}
```

***

# Large Dataset Optimisation

Imagine:

```text
100,000 records
```

Do NOT render all rows.

Use:

```bash
npm install react-window
```

```tsx
import { FixedSizeList }
from "react-window";
```

Render only visible rows.

Complexity:

```text
Without virtualisation:
O(n)

With virtualisation:
O(visible rows)
```

***

# Fuzzy Search (Interview Bonus)

Instead of:

```text
react
```

support:

```text
rct
```

matching:

```text
React
```

Use:

```bash
npm install fuse.js
```

```tsx
import Fuse from "fuse.js";
```

```tsx
const fuse = new Fuse(data, {
  keys: ["title"]
});
```

***

# Accessibility

&lt;input&nbsp; role="combobox"&nbsp; aria-expanded="true"/&gt;&lt;ul role="listbox"&gt;&nbsp; &lt;li role="option"&gt;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; React JS&nbsp; &lt;/li&gt;&lt;/ul&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Support:

* Arrow Up
* Arrow Down
* Enter
* Escape

***

# Senior Frontend Interview Discussion

### Optimisations

* Debouncing
* Memoization (`useMemo`)
* Virtualisation (`react-window`)
* Search result caching (LRU Cache)
* Request cancellation (`AbortController`)

### Architecture

```text
Search Input
      ↓
Debounce
      ↓
LRU Cache
      ↓
Search API
      ↓
Filter Results
      ↓
Highlight Matches
      ↓
Virtualized List
```

### Complexity

Filtering:

```text
O(n)
```

Highlighting:

```text
O(m)
```

Where:

* `n` = number of search results
* `m` = text length

### Production Features

* Fuzzy search
* Infinite scrolling
* Search history
* Recent searches
* Search suggestions
* Keyboard shortcuts
* Highlight matches
* Analytics tracking

This is typically the level of implementation and discussion expected in a **Senior React / Frontend Lead system design interview**.
